title: A Wretched Hive
summary: The heroes travel to Takodana to infiltrate Darga the Hutt's fortress, posing as entertainment to steal the encryption keys to Varth's fortune. They discover a captive Jedi Master and must choose between the mission and their conscience.

gearNotes.reason: Varth coaches the heroes over encrypted comms from outside Darga's palace. Open comlinks in a Hutt fortress means blown cover. Hard permission gate for the entire Long Con sequence.
The entire mission objective is slicing Darga's datapad to extract the encryption keys. Without a proper slicing interface, the core technical challenge is stepped down or impossible.
High-security detention block locks where Jedi Master Denia is held. Without one, bypassing these suffers heavy penalties.
The Tech's audition — ordered to fix Darga's hover-sled with a blaster at their head. No toolkit means stepped-down rolls on a pass/fail moment.
Multi-scene combat from the hit squad through the fortress escape. No medical facilities available.
Underworld social currency in Blackwind Point. Opens doors in criminal circles where credits draw suspicion. Varth's shopping list should include this.
Plant bugs near the Iron Ring delegation or Lady Sinde's private meetings. The intel from these events reveals Project Leviathan's slave labor connection.
Intercepting unencrypted palace communications — security patrol schedules, delivery timings, who talks to whom.
Recording evidence of slave trade and Imperial deals. Leverage material for later adventures.
Heavy combat droid at the filtration plant and elite security droids in the dungeon. Ion damage is the purpose-built counter.
Sealed blast doors during fortress lockdown. Overload Mode cuts through any door as a single action.
Covering movement through hostile corridors during lockdown and the hangar escape.
Quick terminal bypass in the dungeon to access stasis controls. Consumable, single action.
Hangar bay area denial against clustered Stormtroopers.
Multi-scene fighting with no rest. Someone is running on fumes by the hangar escape.

scenes.title: The Welcoming Committee
scenes.readAloud:
You disembark at Blackwind Point — a muddy, desperate shantytown on the far shore of Nymeve Lake. The air is thick with the smell of rotting vegetation and desperation. Refugees huddle in makeshift shelters while criminals eye your gear with open hunger.

The growl of repulsor-bikes cuts through the noise. A swoop gang circles your landing pad, engines revving. Their leader — a scarred human woman with a vibro-blade strapped to her leg — pulls up and kills her engine.

"Welcome to the other side of the lake. Landing tax is five hundred credits. Non-negotiable."
scenes.gmNotes: Kessra and The Vipers demand a landing tax. This sets the tone for Takodana's dark side: might makes right. If the heroes fight and win, locals regard them with respect and fear. If they pay, they're marked as easy targets. Kessra may reappear later as a hired guard in Darga's palace, holding a grudge. Varth has briefed the heroes: they need to find his contact Warrick Raden, a Devaronian fixer who can get them into Darga's court.

npcs.name: Kessra
npcs.notes: Scarred human woman. Leader of The Vipers. May reappear in Darga's fortress.

passive.name: Fleet Footed
passive.description: May take 2 maneuvers per turn instead of 1.

actions.name: Vibroblade Slash
actions.description: Melee attack with vibroblade. Fast and vicious.

actions.name: Drive-By Shot
actions.description: Ranged attack from a moving swoop. +1 zone movement after.

maneuver.name: Reposition
maneuver.description: Move without provoking reactions.

exploit.name: Hit and Fade
exploit.description: After attacking, immediately mount swoop and move 2 zones.

threatBuild.notes: Kessra is a recurring NPC. May reappear as hired muscle in Darga's palace later.

npcs.name: The Vipers
npcs.notes: Armed with blasters and vibro-blades. Intimidation over combat skill.

passive.name: Fleet Footed
passive.description: May take 2 maneuvers per turn.

actions.name: Blaster Snap
actions.description: Ranged attack.

actions.name: Vibroblade Lunge
actions.description: Melee attack.

maneuver.name: Reposition
maneuver.description: Move without provoking reactions.

exploit.name: Scatter
exploit.description: If leader is downed, all Vipers disengage and flee.

threatBuild.notes: Minion gang. Masterful+ = takedown. Paper tigers — flee if Kessra falls.

scenes.hazards: Open landing pad — surrounded by hostile gang. Muddy terrain. Refugees as potential collateral.

decisions.consequence: Marked as easy targets. Pickpockets and grifters will target you.
If successful, the Vipers back down. Local respect earned.
Vipers retreat if beaten. Newfound respect (and fear) from locals. Kessra holds a grudge.

skillChecks.context: Staring down Kessra and the Vipers to avoid paying landing tax.
skillChecks.success: Kessra reads you as dangerous. Backs off with a sneer. Locals take notice.
skillChecks.failure: Kessra doesn't flinch. The Vipers close in.

skillChecks.context: Talking down the situation without violence or payment.
skillChecks.success: Kessra laughs — she likes confidence. Waives the tax "just this once."
skillChecks.failure: Kessra sees through it. Price doubles.

skillChecks.context: Reading Kessra's intentions — shakedown or genuine threat?
skillChecks.success: Pure shakedown. The Vipers are a paper tiger — they won't fight someone who pushes back.
skillChecks.failure: Can't tell. She seems genuinely dangerous.

skillChecks.context: Scanning the landing pad area for tactical details.
skillChecks.success: Vipers positioned to flee, not fight. Their swoops are running — they want a quick exit if things go wrong.
skillChecks.failure: Chaotic scene. Too many variables.

encounters.name: Viper Shakedown
encounters.description: Kessra and 6 Vipers demand 500 credits. Social challenge with combat fallback.
encounters.tactics: Kessra leads verbally. Vipers fan out to cut off exits. If fighting starts, they use swoops for hit-and-run.

environmentMechanics.name: Muddy Landing Pad
environmentMechanics.effect: Difficult terrain. Movement costs double. Swoops ignore this.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Stay on the ferrocrete pad edges.

environmentMechanics.name: Refugee Crowd
environmentMechanics.effect: Civilians scatter. Stray shots risk hitting bystanders.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Controlled fire only. Area attacks cause collateral.

rewards.narrative: Local reputation established — respected or marked as easy targets.

pacing.openingBeat: The Vipers circle the landing pad.
pacing.risingAction: Kessra makes her demand. Tension builds.
pacing.climax: The party's response — pay, talk, or fight.
pacing.resolution: The Vipers withdraw one way or another. The party enters Blackwind Point.

scenes.title: Hunting the Fixer
scenes.readAloud:
The encampment is a maze of tents, grounded starships, and mud-brick hovels. Every face you pass wears the same expression — guarded suspicion.

Through bribes, threats, and persistence, a pattern emerges: Warrick Raden has gone to ground. The nervous Devaronian used to run errands for Darga but vanished from the cantinas recently. Word is he skimmed money from the Hutt and is hiding out at the old Water Filtration Plant on the edge of the swamp.
scenes.gmNotes: A social/investigation scene. The heroes must navigate the underworld of Blackwind Point to find Raden. Local bartenders, refugees, and fences can provide information. The Water Filtration Plant is a rusted industrial facility half-sunk in the bog. This is a good opportunity for roleplaying and gathering intel about Darga's current state — he's become paranoid and obsessed with his new Imperial guests.

npcs.name: Local Contacts
npcs.notes: Bartenders, refugees, fences who can point to Raden's location.

passive.name: Street Smart
passive.description: Knows the local power structure and who to avoid.

actions.name: Tip Off
actions.description: Provide information about a local person or place.

actions.name: Warn Away
actions.description: Social pressure to make strangers leave.

maneuver.name: Gossip Network
maneuver.description: Spread or gather rumors.

threatBuild.notes: Non-combatant NPCs. Social interaction targets only. Represent bartenders, fences, refugees.

scenes.hazards: Pickpockets, grifters, and informants who might tip off Darga's network.

decisions.consequence: Fastest method. Costs credits but gets reliable intel.
Free but may attract attention from Darga's informants.

skillChecks.context: Gathering intel on Raden's location from the encampment.
skillChecks.success: Multiple sources confirm: Water Filtration Plant, edge of the swamp. He's been hiding there 3 days.
skillChecks.failure: Conflicting information. Two locations — one is a trap.

skillChecks.context: Loosening tongues at the cantinas. Buying drinks, making friends.
skillChecks.success: A bartender opens up. Raden was in yesterday, jumpy. Mentioned "the plant."
skillChecks.failure: Everyone clams up. Strangers asking about Raden draws suspicion.

skillChecks.context: Posing as someone Raden owes money to, or as Darga's people.
skillChecks.success: A fence points you to the filtration plant, hoping to collect a finder's fee.
skillChecks.failure: The fence reports your inquiry to Darga's network. Hit squad arrives faster.

skillChecks.context: Avoiding Darga's informants while asking questions.
skillChecks.success: Your inquiries stay below the radar. No alert to Darga.
skillChecks.failure: Someone tips off Darga. The hit squad in Scene 4 arrives 1 round earlier.

skillChecks.context: Reading the encampment's social dynamics. Who runs what.
skillChecks.success: Clear picture: Darga's reach extends through the cantinas but not the refugee camp. The refugees are an information blind spot.
skillChecks.failure: Everyone seems equally untrustworthy.

environmentMechanics.name: Informant Network
environmentMechanics.effect: Darga has eyes in Blackwind Point. Aggressive questioning or mentions of "Varth" trigger alerts.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Use cover identities. Ask about Raden obliquely.

environmentMechanics.name: Refugee Camp Buffer
environmentMechanics.trigger: If heroes investigate the refugee area
environmentMechanics.effect: Refugees distrust everyone equally — harder to get info but also harder for Darga's network to track inquiries here.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Spend time. Build trust. Medicine or supply gifts open doors.

pacing.openingBeat: The encampment is a maze. Every face is guarded.
pacing.risingAction: Leads converge on the Water Filtration Plant.
pacing.climax: The heroes decide how to approach the plant.
pacing.resolution: Raden's location is confirmed. The party moves out.

scenes.title: The Rat in the Trap
scenes.readAloud:
The filtration plant is a noisy, steam-venting structure half-sunk in the bog. You find Warrick Raden hiding in a maintenance shed — a nervous wreck clutching a blaster and jumping at shadows.

When you mention Varth's name, Raden's face cycles through shock, fear, and desperate hope in rapid succession. "Varth? He's alive? Stars help me — he's alive."
scenes.gmNotes: Raden thought Varth was dead. He confirms he can get the heroes into Darga's palace but refuses to go back freely. He reveals that Darga has 'changed' — becoming paranoid and obsessed with Imperial guests. The heroes must de-escalate Raden's panic and convince him to cooperate. He's their ticket into the palace.

npcs.name: Warrick Raden
npcs.notes: Nervous wreck. Owes Varth a debt. The heroes' entry ticket to Darga's palace.

passive.name: Fixer's Contacts
passive.description: Raden knows everyone in Darga's operation. Can identify guards, routines, and weak points.

actions.name: Spill Info
actions.description: Provide palace intel when motivated.

actions.name: Panic Shot
actions.description: Wild blaster shot when cornered. Low accuracy.

maneuver.name: Bolt
maneuver.description: Attempt to flee. Must be intercepted with Athletics or Evasion.

threatBuild.notes: Raden is not a fighter. His value is information, not combat.

scenes.hazards: Steam pipes, unstable industrial equipment. Raden is armed and panicky.

decisions.consequence: He agrees to help if the heroes protect him from Darga's retribution.
He cooperates but may betray the heroes at a critical moment.

skillChecks.context: Calming Raden down. He's panicking with a blaster aimed at you.
skillChecks.success: Raden lowers the blaster. Starts talking. Relieved that Varth is alive.
skillChecks.failure: Raden stays panicky. Blaster stays up. Additional checks needed to de-escalate.

skillChecks.context: Convincing Raden to help get the heroes into Darga's palace.
skillChecks.success: Raden agrees if the heroes promise to protect him from Darga.
skillChecks.failure: Raden wants more — credits, guaranteed extraction, or Darga dead.

skillChecks.context: Threatening Raden into compliance.
skillChecks.success: Raden caves immediately — he's a wreck. But he'll look for ways to betray you later.
skillChecks.failure: Raden's panic overrides his fear. He bolts or shoots wildly.

skillChecks.context: Reading Raden. Is he trustworthy?
skillChecks.success: Genuinely terrified. He owes Varth a real debt. He'll cooperate as long as he believes you can protect him.
skillChecks.failure: Hard to read through the panic. Could be genuine or could be stalling.

skillChecks.context: Searching the maintenance shed for intel.
skillChecks.success: Raden's notes: sketched palace maps, guard shift times, Darga's paranoid habits. Useful intelligence.
skillChecks.failure: Just a panicking man's hideout. Nothing organized.

environmentMechanics.name: Steam-Filled Shed
environmentMechanics.effect: Hissing pipes, condensation, poor visibility at distance. Concealment for anyone more than 1 zone away.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Stay close. Voice carries — social checks are unaffected.

environmentMechanics.name: Armed Panicker
environmentMechanics.effect: Raden has a blaster and twitchy trigger finger. First social failure may result in him firing wildly.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: De-escalate before approaching. Slow movements.

items.name: Palace Maps (sketch)
items.note: Raden's hand-drawn maps of Darga's fortress. Rough but useful.

rewards.narrative: Raden agrees to get the heroes into Darga's court.

pacing.openingBeat: Raden has a blaster aimed at the door.
pacing.risingAction: Negotiation — what does Raden need to cooperate?
pacing.climax: The deal is struck or forced.
pacing.resolution: Raden commits to the palace infiltration plan.

scenes.title: The Hit Squad
scenes.readAloud:
The negotiation is interrupted by the sound of heavy boots on metal. Through the grimy windows, you see movement — Trandoshan Mercenaries, heavily armed, surrounding the filtration plant. A heavy combat droid stomps into position at the main entrance.

Darga has grown tired of waiting for Raden to come home.
scenes.gmNotes: Darga sent a hit squad to silence Raden. Trandoshan Mercenaries and a heavy combat droid besiege the shed. The fight takes place in cramped machinery — steam pipes burst, obscuring vision. The Trandoshans use the environment to hunt. The heroes MUST protect Raden — if he dies, their easy route into the palace dies with him.

npcs.name: Trandoshan Mercenaries
npcs.notes: Hired guns. Using the industrial environment to hunt.

passive.name: Unrelenting
passive.description: Ignore the first [Weakened] condition per scene.

actions.name: Claw Slash
actions.description: Melee attack with natural claws. +1 Power.

actions.name: Hunting Rifle
actions.description: Ranged attack at medium range.

maneuver.name: Scent Track
maneuver.description: In obscured conditions, locate one hidden enemy. Ignores concealment from steam.

exploit.name: Regeneration
exploit.description: At end of turn, heal 1 Vitality. Trandoshan biology.

threatBuild.notes: Standard Brutes. Tough = ignore Fleeting damage. Scent Track bypasses steam concealment. A real fight.

npcs.name: Heavy Combat Droid
npcs.notes: Blocking the main entrance. Heavy armor and weapons.

passive.name: Unrelenting
passive.description: Ignore the first [Weakened] condition per scene.

actions.name: Heavy Blaster Cannon
actions.description: Ranged attack with integrated heavy blaster. +1 Power.

actions.name: Battering Ram
actions.description: Charge + melee. Destroys cover.

maneuver.name: Advance
maneuver.description: Move forward 1 zone. Ignores difficult terrain.

gambit.name: Suppressive Barrage
gambit.description: Area fire. All targets in zone gain [Suppressed].

exploit.name: Overload
exploit.description: When reduced to 0 Vitality, explodes. [Hazard 3] in zone.

threatBuild.notes: Elite Brute Droid. Defense 8 = max(5,1)-1+2+3(heavy armor)-1. Iron Clad: uses Defense vs Evasion attacks. 5. Vulnerable to Ion damage. The DPS check of the encounter.

scenes.hazards: Cramped industrial machinery. Bursting steam pipes (obscure vision, scald damage). Collapsing catwalks.

decisions.consequence: Protect Raden but risk being overwhelmed in a fixed position.
Must punch through the Trandoshans to reach an exit. Raden is vulnerable during movement.

skillChecks.context: Reading the hit squad's approach pattern.
skillChecks.success: They're coming from three directions — rear maintenance hatch is unguarded.
skillChecks.failure: No clear picture. React to contact.

skillChecks.context: Overloading the filtration machinery for area denial.
skillChecks.success: Steam erupts in a wall of scalding vapor. Trandoshans are forced back 2 rounds.
skillChecks.failure: Machinery sputters. Partial obstruction only.

skillChecks.context: Collapsing catwalks onto the attacking Trandoshans.
skillChecks.success: Catwalk falls. Two Trandoshans are pinned under wreckage.
skillChecks.failure: Catwalk buckles but holds. Creates difficult terrain only.

skillChecks.context: Treating Raden if he takes fire during the ambush.
skillChecks.success: Raden is patched up and mobile.
skillChecks.failure: Raden is alive but badly hurt. Must be carried.

encounters.name: Trandoshan Ambush
encounters.description: 4 Trandoshan Mercenaries breach from multiple entry points. Heavy Combat Droid blocks the main exit.
encounters.tactics: Trandoshans use scent-tracking in the steam. The droid advances slowly, absorbing fire. Protect Raden.

environmentMechanics.name: Industrial Steam
environmentMechanics.effect: Scalding steam fills a zone. [Hazard 1] per round. Concealment.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Breath mask reduces to no damage. Stay low.

environmentMechanics.name: Cramped Machinery
environmentMechanics.effect: Narrow corridors between machinery. No flanking. Limits movement to single-file.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Use chokepoints defensively.

environmentMechanics.name: Collapsible Catwalks
environmentMechanics.effect: Overhead catwalks can be dropped. Pins targets underneath. Difficult terrain.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Don't stand under catwalks during firefights.

items.name: Trandoshan Weapons
items.note: Scavenged medium blasters and a hunting rifle.

rewards.narrative: Raden survives. Hit squad eliminated. Path to the palace is clear.

pacing.openingBeat: Heavy boots on metal. Shadows in the windows.
pacing.risingAction: Trandoshans breach from multiple points. Steam and chaos.
pacing.climax: The heavy combat droid advances. Must be dealt with to escape.
pacing.resolution: Hit squad is down. Raden is shaken but alive.

scenes.title: The Proposition
scenes.readAloud:
With the hit squad defeated, Raden is terrified but alive. He realizes he can never stay in the encampment. His eyes dart to your weapons, to the door, and finally settle on acceptance.

"Alright. I know how to get you in. Darga is hosting a delegation of high rollers and business partners. He wants entertainment — gladiators, gamblers, mercenaries. I'll present you to the Hutt as 'prime talent.' But once we're inside..." He swallows hard. "...you're on your own."
scenes.gmNotes: Raden proposes presenting the heroes as entertainment for Darga's court. Options: gladiators for his arena, high-stakes gamblers, or specialized mercenaries. He warns that Darga is hosting Imperial Mining Guild members — heroes must hide political affiliations. The skiff ride across Nymeve Lake to Darga's fortress is atmospheric: the moss-covered stone ruin retrofitted with gaudy tech and heavy weapons looms through the mist.

npcs.name: Warrick Raden
npcs.notes: Reluctant guide. Will present the heroes as entertainment.

passive.name: Fixer's Contacts
passive.description: Raden knows everyone in Darga's operation. Can identify guards, routines, and weak points.

actions.name: Spill Info
actions.description: Provide palace intel when motivated.

actions.name: Panic Shot
actions.description: Wild blaster shot when cornered. Low accuracy.

maneuver.name: Bolt
maneuver.description: Attempt to flee. Must be intercepted with Athletics or Evasion.

threatBuild.notes: Raden is not a fighter. His value is information, not combat. (Recurring — same stat block as earlier scene.)

scenes.hazards: Crossing Nymeve Lake by skiff. Darga's fortress visible through the mist.

decisions.consequence: Physical challenge. Must prove combat prowess in Darga's arena.
Social/mental challenge. High-stakes games in Darga's court.
Most flexible cover but requires convincing backstory.

narrativeLinks.note: The heroes' chosen cover determines their audition in Darga's throne room.

skillChecks.context: Building a convincing cover identity for the palace infiltration.
skillChecks.success: Air-tight cover. Raden vouches convincingly.
skillChecks.failure: Cover has gaps. Darga's people may ask uncomfortable questions.

skillChecks.context: Reassuring Raden during the skiff ride. He's getting cold feet.
skillChecks.success: Raden steadies. He'll play his part.
skillChecks.failure: Raden is visibly nervous. Darga's court may notice.

skillChecks.context: Reading the lake conditions during the skiff crossing.
skillChecks.success: Notice: the lake is patrolled by aquatic predators. Useful if escape requires the water route.
skillChecks.failure: Unremarkable crossing.

environmentMechanics.name: Nymeve Lake Crossing
environmentMechanics.effect: Open water. No cover. If attacked, swimming in Takodana's lake is dangerous — aquatic predators.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Stay on the skiff. Don't fall in.

rewards.narrative: Cover identities established. Entry to Darga's court secured.

pacing.openingBeat: Raden outlines the plan. The party chooses their cover.
pacing.risingAction: The skiff ride across the misty lake. The fortress looms.
pacing.climax: The fortress gates open. No turning back.
pacing.resolution: The heroes enter Darga's court under false pretenses.

parts.title: The Court of the Crime Lord

scenes.title: The Audition
scenes.subtitle: The Throne Room
scenes.readAloud:
The heroes are led through damp, torch-lit corridors that smell of mold and roasting meat. You emerge into Darga's Throne Room — a sensory assault of thick smoke, frantic jizz-wailer music, and a crowd of sycophants, thieves, and scavengers.

At the far end, resting on a massive reinforced hover-sled, sits Darga the Hutt. He is bloated, scarred, and flanked by a polished silver protocol droid. Darga barks in Huttese, his deep voice shaking the room. The droid translates:

"The Mighty Darga is bored with beggars. Prove you are worth his air."
scenes.gmNotes: Darga demands an immediate demonstration. The Muscle gets a fight against a bodyguard or beast. The Face gets a battle of wits. The Tech gets ordered to fix something on Darga's hover-sled with a blaster at their head. If heroes succeed, Darga welcomes them as 'guests' (prisoners with privileges). He then turns on Raden — ordering Gamorreans to drag him to the dungeons as punishment. Raden's screams serve as a warning of the price of failure.

npcs.name: Darga the Hutt
npcs.notes: Bloated, scarred, paranoid. Sits on an electrum-plated hover-sled. Never speaks Basic — uses TC-663.

passive.name: Absolute Authority
passive.description: All allies in Darga's court gain +1 to Resist social attacks. His presence is law.

actions.name: Command
actions.description: Order one ally to immediately attack or take an action.

actions.name: Crushing Tail
actions.description: Melee attack at engaged range. +2 Power. Hutt mass.

maneuver.name: Hover-Sled
maneuver.description: Move on repulsor sled. Not impeded by terrain.

gambit.name: Example
gambit.description: Order guards to execute a prisoner or destroy property. All witnesses gain [Shaken].

exploit.name: Bounty
exploit.description: Declare a bounty on one target. All mercenaries in the fortress gain +1 Power vs that target for the scene.

threatBuild.notes: Darga is a crime lord, not a fighter. Boss x2 Vitality = 24 = (5+4+3)*2. Power 7 via Commander (Presence 5-1)+3. He fights through his army. Direct combat is a mistake — he's a Hutt on a hover-sled surrounded by guards.

npcs.name: TC-663 ('Six-Six')
npcs.notes: Darga's translator. Secretly an agent of Switch from Adventure 1.

passive.name: Double Agent
passive.description: TC-663 serves Switch. If heroes use Switch's code phrase, he provides palace maps and datapad location.

actions.name: Translate
actions.description: Translate Huttese. Can subtly alter translations to help or hinder.

actions.name: Whispered Intel
actions.description: Pass information discreetly during court proceedings.

maneuver.name: Blend In
maneuver.description: As a protocol droid, TC-663 is invisible to court security. Can move freely.

threatBuild.notes: Non-combatant spy. His real value is turning him into an asset using Switch's code phrase from Adventure 1.

npcs.name: Igren Demos
npcs.notes: Darga's right hand. Secretly Force-sensitive and plotting a coup.

passive.name: Untrained Sensitivity
passive.description: Demos can sense Force disturbances but cannot control it. He senses Denia's awakening and any Force use in the palace.

actions.name: Force Slam
actions.description: Crude Force push. Targets Endure. Knocks target back 1 zone.

actions.name: Vibro-Staff Strike
actions.description: Melee attack with vibro-staff.

maneuver.name: Force Pull
maneuver.description: Yank one object or person 1 zone closer.

exploit.name: Desperate Surge
exploit.description: Once per encounter, double Power on a Force attack. Costs 2 Vitality.

threatBuild.notes: Elite mini-boss. 5. Untrained Force user — dangerous but sloppy. Plotting to usurp Darga.

npcs.name: Gamorrean Guards
npcs.notes: Vibro-axe wielding palace guards.

passive.name: Unrelenting
passive.description: Ignore first [Weakened] per scene.

actions.name: Vibro-Axe Chop
actions.description: Melee attack. +1 Power.

actions.name: Headbutt
actions.description: Close range. Target must Endure or gain [Stunned].

maneuver.name: Intimidating Advance
maneuver.description: Move + target resists or gains [Shaken].

exploit.name: Berserk
exploit.description: When below half Vitality, +1 Power on all attacks.

threatBuild.notes: Minion Brutes. Tough. Masterful+ = takedown. Palace guards — vibro-axes and bad attitudes.

scenes.hazards: Hostile court environment. Surrounded by Darga's forces. No easy escape.

decisions.choice: Impress Darga with a spectacular audition
decisions.consequence: Earn 'guest' status and freedom to move within the palace.

decisions.choice: Try to intervene when Raden is taken
decisions.consequence: Extremely dangerous — would blow cover immediately. Darga's court is heavily armed.

skillChecks.context: The Muscle's audition — fighting a Gamorrean in Darga's pit.
skillChecks.success: Decisive victory. Darga is impressed. [Guest] status earned.
skillChecks.failure: A sloppy showing. Darga is unimpressed. Cover is thinner.

skillChecks.context: The Face's audition — a battle of wits with Darga's courtiers.
skillChecks.success: Darga laughs. He likes you. The court takes notice.
skillChecks.failure: Flat performance. Darga yawns. Cover survives but barely.

skillChecks.context: The Tech's audition — fixing Darga's hover-sled with a blaster at your head.
skillChecks.success: Sled purrs like new. Darga is delighted. Gives you run of the workshop.
skillChecks.failure: Sled sputters. Not broken worse, but Darga is skeptical.

skillChecks.context: Maintaining cover identity under Darga's scrutiny.
skillChecks.success: Darga buys it completely. No further questions.
skillChecks.failure: Darga is suspicious. Igren Demos is ordered to watch you closely.

skillChecks.context: Reading the court dynamics — who has power, who is plotting.
skillChecks.success: TC-663 keeps glancing at you meaningfully. Igren Demos watches Darga like a predator.
skillChecks.failure: Overwhelming sensory chaos. Too many players to read.

encounters.name: The Audition
encounters.description: Each PC must prove their worth to Darga in their specialty. Muscle fights, Face talks, Tech fixes.
encounters.tactics: Darga is bored and jaded. Only genuine skill or entertainment impresses him.

environmentMechanics.name: Hostile Court
environmentMechanics.effect: Surrounded by Darga's forces. No weapons allowed in the throne room (confiscated at the door). Violence = instant death.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Play the part. Do not break cover.

environmentMechanics.name: Darga's Attention Span
environmentMechanics.effect: Darga loses interest quickly. Failed auditions don't cause immediate expulsion but reduce trust.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Make it entertaining. Darga values spectacle over competence.

rewards.narrative: "Guest" status in Darga's court — freedom to move within approved areas.

pacing.openingBeat: Led through damp corridors into the sensory assault of Darga's throne room.
pacing.climax: Darga's judgment. Then Raden is dragged to the dungeon.
pacing.resolution: The heroes are "welcomed" to court. The clock starts.

scenes.title: The Long Con
scenes.subtitle: The Court
scenes.readAloud:
Over the next day cycle, you navigate the dangerous politics of Darga's court. Admiral Varth coaches you over encrypted comms, pointing out targets and reminding you to keep your eyes on the prize.

Three events unfold that reveal the true scale of what you've stumbled into...
scenes.gmNotes:
Three key events during the 'free roaming' phase:

1. THE IRON RING DELEGATION: Trandoshan Slavers drag chained Nazren prisoners into court, arguing about payment. Eavesdropping reveals Darga supplies slave labor for Project Leviathan.

2. THE IMPERIAL BUYER: Lady Sinde arrives — a severe logistics officer for Project Leviathan. Varth recognizes her voice. She's negotiating the next shipment.

3. THE DROID RACE: Darga announces a droid race to celebrate his deal. This is the heroes' best chance — while Darga is distracted gambling, they can slip away to slice the datapad or explore secure areas.

TC-663 can be turned if heroes treat him with respect or use Switch's code phrase — he'll provide palace maps and datapad location.

npcs.name: Iron Ring Slavers
npcs.notes: Arguing about 'damaged merchandise' (dying slaves). Led by a brutal Zabrak.

passive.name: Implied Violence
passive.description: Social attacks inflict [Shaken] instead of normal social conditions.

actions.name: Slaver's Lash
actions.description: Melee attack with shock-whip. Targets Endure.

actions.name: Threaten
actions.description: Social attack using implied force. Targets Resist.

maneuver.name: Loom
maneuver.description: Move to close range. Target resists or gains [Rattled].

gambit.name: Crack the Whip
gambit.description: Area social attack. All witnesses gain [Shaken].

exploit.name: Chain Gang
exploit.description: If a target is [Prone], automatically apply [Restrained].

threatBuild.notes: Enforcer slavers. Fearless: immune to [Shaken]/[Rattled]. Loathsome but dangerous.

npcs.name: Lady Sinde
npcs.notes: Project Leviathan buyer. Severe, authoritative, views Darga as a necessary tool.

passive.name: Imperial Authority
passive.description: Lady Sinde commands Stormtroopers. Her orders are obeyed without question.

actions.name: Direct Fire
actions.description: Order a Stormtrooper to attack immediately.

actions.name: Cutting Assessment
actions.description: Social attack that dismantles cover stories. Targets Resist.

maneuver.name: Issue Orders
maneuver.description: One ally gains a bonus maneuver.

gambit.name: Ultimatum
gambit.description: Target must comply or be marked for elimination.

exploit.name: Call Reinforcements
exploit.description: Summon 4 Stormtrooper minions to her location.

threatBuild.notes: Commander. Resist 5 = Grit 3-1+2+1(disciplined). She is a logistics officer, not a frontline fighter. Her danger is the army behind her.

npcs.name: Gome
npcs.notes: Elderly palace accountant. Doesn't care about crime — loves numbers. Can explain Darga's Imperial shipments.

passive.name: Loves Numbers
passive.description: Gome will talk endlessly about financial details if asked. He doesn't care about crime — only about elegant accounting.

actions.name: Financial Breakdown
actions.description: Explain Darga's shipping operations in excruciating detail.

actions.name: Shrug
actions.description: Deflect moral questions with bureaucratic indifference.

maneuver.name: Pull Records
maneuver.description: Access accounting data from memory.

threatBuild.notes: Non-combatant. The only NPC who will freely discuss Darga's operations — he just doesn't see the moral dimension.

scenes.hazards: Court politics — any misstep can blow the heroes' cover. Imperial presence increases stakes.

decisions.choice: Use the droid race as cover to find the datapad
decisions.consequence: Best window of opportunity. Darga is distracted.
If successful, he provides palace maps and the datapad's exact location.
Moral victory but extremely dangerous — could blow cover and start a war.

narrativeLinks.note: Lady Sinde's presence connects to the Imperial deal at the Cloud City tournament.

skillChecks.context: Maintaining cover during the court events.
skillChecks.success: Cover holds perfectly throughout the day cycle.
skillChecks.failure: A suspicious courtier begins asking pointed questions. Demos takes notice.

skillChecks.context: Gathering intel during the Iron Ring delegation arrival.
skillChecks.success: Overhear: Darga supplies slave labor for "Project Leviathan." Shipping manifests mention Bespin.
skillChecks.failure: Caught only fragments. Know slaves are involved but not the destination.

skillChecks.context: Eavesdropping on Lady Sinde's private meeting with Darga.
skillChecks.success: Full conversation: next shipment coordinates, payment terms, mention of "the Accountant's records."
skillChecks.failure: Spotted by a guard. Must talk your way out — Deception check or cover blown.

skillChecks.context: Reading Lady Sinde's relationship with Darga.
skillChecks.success: She despises Darga. He's a tool. The Empire is already planning to cut him loose once Project Leviathan is complete.
skillChecks.failure: Professional demeanor. Hard to read.

skillChecks.context: Turning TC-663 into an asset using Switch's code phrase.
skillChecks.success: TC-663's photoreceptors flash recognition. He provides palace maps, datapad location, and guard rotations.
skillChecks.failure: TC-663 is cautious. Provides partial information only.
skillChecks.narrativePacing: Key check. The code phrase from Adv1 reduces this to T0/P0.

skillChecks.context: Slicing Darga's datapad during the droid race distraction.
skillChecks.success: Encryption keys extracted cleanly. No trace left.
skillChecks.failure: Partial extraction. Silent alarm triggers. Lockdown in 5 rounds.

encounters.name: The Droid Race
encounters.description: A droid race through the palace corridors. Darga gambles heavily. The heroes' window to act.
encounters.tactics: While Darga watches the race, security is reduced. Best chance to slice the datapad or explore restricted areas.

environmentMechanics.name: Court Surveillance
environmentMechanics.effect: Darga's court has eyes everywhere. Extended absence or unusual behavior is noticed.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Coordinate with TC-663. Use the droid race as cover.

environmentMechanics.name: The Droid Race Window
environmentMechanics.effect: Security reduced for 20 minutes (game time). Guards distracted by the race.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Use this window for the datapad heist.

items.name: Encryption Keys
items.note: Varth's fortune access codes — if the heist succeeds.

rewards.narrative: Intelligence gathered. Heist attempted. The adventure's fate pivots here.

pacing.openingBeat: A day in Darga's court. Three events unfold.
pacing.risingAction: Iron Ring slavers arrive. Lady Sinde negotiates. TC-663 can be turned.
pacing.climax: The droid race — the heroes' window to act.
pacing.resolution: Heist succeeds or fails. Either way, the next scene is triggered.

scenes.title: The Complication
scenes.subtitle: The Dungeon
scenes.readAloud:
Using the cover of the droid race, you slip into the restricted lower levels. Instead of a vault, you find a high-security detention block guarded by elite droids.

Inside the most secure cell is a stasis pod hooked to life-support machinery. Through the frost-covered viewport, you see a human woman in tattered Jedi robes, suspended in a Force-induced coma. Her eyes are closed, but you feel something — a whisper at the edge of your consciousness.

Admiral Varth's voice turns cold over the comms: "Leave her. She is a liability. If you wake a Jedi, every Inquisitor in the sector will hunt us down. Get the keys and get out."
scenes.gmNotes: This is the campaign's first major Moral Event Horizon. Jedi Master Denia was found by Darga after Order 66 and kept as a trophy to auction to the Inquisitorius. She's weak, barely alive, and telepathically reaching out to the heroes. Varth is furious — a Jedi is a heat magnet. This decision shapes the rest of the campaign. Saving Denia brings the Inquisitorius down on the heroes but gains them a powerful ally. Leaving her preserves the 'scoundrel' path but at a moral cost.

npcs.name: Jedi Master Denia
npcs.notes: In stasis for 3 years since Order 66. Powerful but frail. Doesn't know the Empire exists yet.

passive.name: Weakened Master
passive.description: Denia's stats represent her full potential. Currently: all physical actions [Weakened], Vitality halved (4), single action per round. Recovers over Adventures 4-9.

actions.name: Force Barrier
actions.description: Create a telekinetic shield. Allies in zone gain [Cover 2] for 1 round.

actions.name: Telepathic Bond
actions.description: Communicate silently with one target. Share images, emotions, warnings.

maneuver.name: Sense Danger
maneuver.description: Detect hostile intent within the scene. Provides tactical warning.

exploit.name: Desperate Burst
exploit.description: Once per adventure, unleash full Force power. All enemies in zone gain [Stunned]. Denia collapses afterward.

threatBuild.notes: Denia is a Jedi Master at full power — but she has been in stasis for 3 years. Current effective stats are halved. She recovers gradually through the campaign. She is an NPC ally, not under player control.

npcs.name: Elite Guard Droids
npcs.notes: Guarding the high-security detention block.

passive.name: Disciplined Fire
passive.description: +1 Power on weapon attacks.

actions.name: Integrated Blaster
actions.description: Built-in medium blaster. Cannot be disarmed.

actions.name: Stun Shock
actions.description: Melee. Target must Endure or gain [Stunned].

maneuver.name: Lock Down
maneuver.description: Seal one door or access point in the zone.

gambit.name: Overwatch Protocol
gambit.description: Hold action. Fire on first movement in line of sight.

exploit.name: Alert Network
exploit.description: Trigger palace-wide alarm. All security responds.

threatBuild.notes: Iron Clad security droids. Defense 6 = max(3,3)-1+2+2(medium armor). Vulnerable to Ion damage — Ion Grenade drops them instantly.

scenes.hazards: High-security zone. Silent alarms if stasis machinery is tampered with. Igren Demos can sense Force disturbances.

decisions.consequence: Triggers silent alarm. Igren Demos senses the Force disturbance. Inquisitors will hunt the heroes. But gains a powerful ally.
Preserves cover for a clean getaway. But abandons a helpless prisoner to torture and sale.

narrativeLinks.note: Rescuing Denia (or failing the datapad heist) triggers the fortress lockdown.

skillChecks.context: Slipping into the restricted lower levels during the droid race.
skillChecks.success: Clean entry. No guards notice.
skillChecks.failure: A guard sees movement. Must be dealt with quietly.

skillChecks.context: Disabling the stasis pod's life support to free Denia.
skillChecks.success: Clean disconnect. Denia wakes slowly, disoriented but alive.
skillChecks.failure: Alarm triggers. Igren Demos senses the Force disturbance. Lockdown imminent.

skillChecks.context: Bypassing the high-security cell locks.
skillChecks.success: Locks open silently. No alarm.
skillChecks.failure: Lock triggers backup alarm. Guards dispatched.

skillChecks.context: Stabilizing Denia after 3 years in stasis.
skillChecks.success: Denia is weak but mobile. Can walk with support.
skillChecks.failure: Denia is barely conscious. Must be carried. All movement checks are stepped up.

skillChecks.context: Resisting Denia's telepathic plea — it's emotionally overwhelming.
skillChecks.success: You feel her pain but maintain composure. Clear decision-making.
skillChecks.failure: The emotional weight is staggering. [Rattled] until you act on it or leave.

encounters.name: Elite Guard Droids
encounters.trigger: If alarm triggers or droids detect intruders
encounters.description: Two elite security droids activate and engage. Heavy armor, integrated weapons.
encounters.tactics: Droids fight methodically. Ion weapons are extremely effective.

environmentMechanics.name: Stasis Machinery
environmentMechanics.effect: Disconnecting improperly kills Denia. Tech check required.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Careful technical work. Slicer Spike provides +1 tier on the check.

environmentMechanics.name: Force Echo
environmentMechanics.effect: Force-sensitive characters feel nausea and dread. Igren Demos senses the disturbance from anywhere in the palace.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Nothing. This is unavoidable if Denia is freed.

rewards.narrative: Jedi Master Denia rescued or abandoned. Campaign-defining moment.

pacing.openingBeat: The restricted lower levels. Cold, dark, humming machinery.
pacing.risingAction: Discovery of the stasis pod. Denia's telepathic plea.
pacing.climax: The choice: rescue or leave. Varth argues against it.
pacing.resolution: The consequences begin — alarm, pursuit, or clean getaway.

scenes.title: The Turn
scenes.subtitle: The Alarm
scenes.readAloud:
Klaxons scream through the stone corridors. The fortress goes into lockdown — blast doors grinding shut, red emergency lighting flooding every hall.

In the Throne Room above, Darga's booming voice shakes the walls:

"SPIES! They are Varth's ghosts! KILL THEM ALL!"

Darga's hover-sled engages its repulsors as he flees toward his private hangar bay, clutching the datapad with the encryption keys.
scenes.gmNotes: The alarm is triggered either by rescuing Denia (stasis disconnect + Demos sensing the Force) or by a failed datapad heist. The fortress is now hostile. Heroes in the dungeon are blocked by blast doors. Heroes in the court are surrounded by Gamorreans and Trandoshans. Darga is fleeing with the datapad. The heist has become a fight for survival and a race to catch the Hutt.

npcs.name: Darga the Hutt
npcs.notes: Retreating to his hangar with the encryption keys.

passive.name: Absolute Authority
passive.description: All allies in Darga's court gain +1 to Resist social attacks. His presence is law.

actions.name: Command
actions.description: Order one ally to immediately attack or take an action.

actions.name: Crushing Tail
actions.description: Melee attack at engaged range. +2 Power. Hutt mass.

maneuver.name: Hover-Sled
maneuver.description: Move on repulsor sled. Not impeded by terrain.

gambit.name: Example
gambit.description: Order guards to execute a prisoner or destroy property. All witnesses gain [Shaken].

exploit.name: Bounty
exploit.description: Declare a bounty on one target. All mercenaries in the fortress gain +1 Power vs that target for the scene.

threatBuild.notes: Darga is a crime lord, not a fighter. Boss x2 Vitality = 24 = (5+4+3)*2. Power 7 via Commander (Presence 5-1)+3. He fights through his army. Direct combat is a mistake — he's a Hutt on a hover-sled surrounded by guards. (Recurring — same stat block as earlier scene.)

npcs.name: Gamorrean Guards
npcs.notes: Palace guards responding to lockdown.

passive.name: Unrelenting
passive.description: Ignore first [Weakened] per scene.

actions.name: Vibro-Axe Chop
actions.description: Melee attack. +1 Power.

actions.name: Headbutt
actions.description: Close range. Target must Endure or gain [Stunned].

maneuver.name: Intimidating Advance
maneuver.description: Move + target resists or gains [Shaken].

exploit.name: Berserk
exploit.description: When below half Vitality, +1 Power on all attacks.

threatBuild.notes: Minion Brutes. Tough. Masterful+ = takedown. Palace guards — vibro-axes and bad attitudes. (Recurring — same stat block as earlier scene.)

npcs.name: Trandoshan Mercenaries
npcs.notes: Darga's hired muscle.

passive.name: Unrelenting
passive.description: Ignore the first [Weakened] condition per scene.

actions.name: Claw Slash
actions.description: Melee attack with natural claws. +1 Power.

actions.name: Hunting Rifle
actions.description: Ranged attack at medium range.

maneuver.name: Scent Track
maneuver.description: In obscured conditions, locate one hidden enemy. Ignores concealment from steam.

exploit.name: Regeneration
exploit.description: At end of turn, heal 1 Vitality. Trandoshan biology.

threatBuild.notes: Standard Brutes. Tough = ignore Fleeting damage. Scent Track bypasses steam concealment. A real fight. (Recurring — same stat block.)

scenes.hazards: Fortress lockdown — blast doors sealing, hostile forces converging. Darga is escaping with the objective.

decisions.consequence: Race through hostile corridors. Must fight through Demos first.
Escape with Denia but lose the encryption keys. Darga gets away.

skillChecks.context: Forcing through blast doors during lockdown.
skillChecks.success: Door forced open. Path cleared.
skillChecks.failure: Door holds. Must find alternate route — costs time.

skillChecks.context: Hacking lockdown controls to open sealed corridors.
skillChecks.success: Blast doors retract. Clear path to the hangar wing.
skillChecks.failure: Partial override. Some doors open, others stay sealed.

skillChecks.context: Dodging through crossfire in the corridors.
skillChecks.success: Clean movement. No hits taken.
skillChecks.failure: Caught in a crossfire. Take ranged damage.

skillChecks.context: Finding the fastest route through the hostile fortress.
skillChecks.success: Optimal path identified. Avoid 2 guard stations.
skillChecks.failure: Must fight through every checkpoint.

encounters.name: Fortress Lockdown
encounters.description: Running battle through corridors. Gamorreans and Trandoshans converge. Darga is escaping.
encounters.tactics: Enemies come in waves. Speed is more valuable than kills. Every round spent fighting is a round Darga gains.

environmentMechanics.name: Blast Door Lockdown
environmentMechanics.effect: Sealed durasteel doors block major corridors. Athletics or Tech to bypass.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Fusion Cutter cuts through in 1 action. Tech hack opens them remotely.

environmentMechanics.name: Converging Guards
environmentMechanics.effect: Additional minion guards arrive every 2 rounds.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Move fast. Don't get bogged down in fights.

rewards.narrative: Path to the hangar or escape route secured.

pacing.openingBeat: Klaxons. Red light. Darga's voice booming through the corridors.
pacing.risingAction: Running fight through sealed corridors. Guards converge.
pacing.climax: Demos blocks the path. Or the heroes bypass him.
pacing.resolution: Either confronting Demos or reaching the hangar.

scenes.title: The Majordomo's Stand
scenes.readAloud:
Blocking your path stands Igren Demos, the Neimoidian Majordomo. He is no longer simpering. He holds a vibro-staff with lethal confidence, flanked by his elite Trandoshan bodyguards.

His red eyes widen as he senses the Jedi behind you. "The Jedi... she's awake. Give her to me. I will take her to the Inquisitorius myself and claim what Darga never could."
scenes.gmNotes: Demos is the boss fight of this location. He reveals his Force-sensitivity (untrained) and uses minor Force powers — Force Slam to separate heroes, Force Push to knock them into environmental hazards. His elite Trandoshan bodyguards provide additional threat. Varth screams over comms that Darga is getting away — Demos is stalling. The fight must be decisive.

npcs.name: Igren Demos
npcs.notes: Untrained Force-sensitive. Uses vibro-staff and minor Force powers. Wants to claim Denia for the Inquisitorius.

passive.name: Untrained Sensitivity
passive.description: Demos can sense Force disturbances but cannot control it. He senses Denia's awakening and any Force use in the palace.

actions.name: Force Slam
actions.description: Crude Force push. Targets Endure. Knocks target back 1 zone.

actions.name: Vibro-Staff Strike
actions.description: Melee attack with vibro-staff.

maneuver.name: Force Pull
maneuver.description: Yank one object or person 1 zone closer.

exploit.name: Desperate Surge
exploit.description: Once per encounter, double Power on a Force attack. Costs 2 Vitality.

threatBuild.notes: Elite mini-boss. 5. Untrained Force user — dangerous but sloppy. Plotting to usurp Darga. (Recurring — same stat block as earlier scene.)

npcs.name: Trandoshan Bodyguards
npcs.notes: Demos's personal muscle.

passive.name: Unrelenting
passive.description: Ignore first [Weakened] per scene.

actions.name: Vibroblade Chop
actions.description: Heavy melee attack. +1 Power.

actions.name: Claw Rake
actions.description: Unarmed attack. Natural weapons.

maneuver.name: Shield Demos
maneuver.description: Interpose between Demos and attacker. Take the hit instead.

exploit.name: Regeneration
exploit.description: Heal 1 Vitality at end of turn.

threatBuild.notes: Standard Brutes. Same stats as Scene 4 Trandoshans. Bodyguard behavior — they protect Demos.

scenes.hazards: Force powers — Slam and Push. Environmental hazards (braziers, statues). Narrow corridors favor Demos's staff.

decisions.consequence: Tough fight but clears the path. Risk: Darga gains more escape time.

decisions.choice: Split the party — some fight, some chase Darga
decisions.consequence: Risky but potentially catches Darga. Smaller groups are more vulnerable.

skillChecks.context: Resisting Demos's Force Slam.
skillChecks.success: Stand your ground. The Force push washes over you.
skillChecks.failure: Knocked back 1 zone. [Disoriented] for 1 round.

skillChecks.context: Reading Demos's Force abilities — is he trained?
skillChecks.success: Untrained. Powerful raw talent but no discipline. His attacks are predictable if you know the pattern.
skillChecks.failure: Terrifying display. His abilities seem formidable.

skillChecks.context: Convincing Demos to stand down — Darga has already abandoned him.
skillChecks.success: Demos hesitates. Realizes Darga is fleeing without him. Steps aside in bitter rage.
skillChecks.failure: Demos is too far gone. Fights to the death.

encounters.name: Igren Demos Showdown
encounters.trigger: Heroes encounter Demos in the corridor
encounters.description: Demos with vibro-staff and minor Force powers, flanked by 2 Trandoshan bodyguards. Mini-boss fight.
encounters.tactics: Demos uses Force Slam to separate the party. Bodyguards flank isolated targets. Focus on the strongest-looking PC first.

environmentMechanics.name: Narrow Corridor
environmentMechanics.effect: Only 2 characters can engage Demos simultaneously. Limits flanking.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Use ranged attacks from behind the front line.

environmentMechanics.name: Force Hazards
environmentMechanics.effect: Braziers and statues become projectiles. Environmental damage in the zone.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Take cover behind pillars. Force projectiles are indirect.

rewards.narrative: Demos is defeated or persuaded to stand down. Path to the hangar is clear.

pacing.openingBeat: Demos blocks the corridor. He is no longer simpering.
pacing.risingAction: Force powers and bodyguards. A real fight.
pacing.climax: Demos is cornered — fight or talk?
pacing.resolution: Demos falls or surrenders. The hangar awaits.

scenes.title: The Hangar Bay
scenes.readAloud:
You burst through the blast doors into the humid, open-air hangar. You're too late — Darga's massive luxury yacht, the Glorious Chariot, is already hovering on repulsors, engines flaring blue-white. The boarding ramp retracts as the gold-and-purple hull begins to rise.

But it's not just Darga. Lady Sinde has ordered her Stormtrooper escort to secure the hangar and eliminate all witnesses.

Blaster fire erupts from behind crates of Tibanna gas as white-armored figures take firing positions.
scenes.gmNotes: Darga escapes in the Glorious Chariot, collapsing part of the ceiling to discourage pursuit. Lady Sinde's Stormtroopers are eliminating witnesses to protect Project Leviathan's secrecy. The heroes fight across the hangar using crates and equipment for cover. If fast enough, they can reach their ship and launch. They see Darga's yacht rocket toward the atmosphere and jump to hyperspace. Varth gets a partial trajectory lock — the vector aligns with the Anoat Sector. Bespin. Cloud City.

npcs.name: Lady Sinde
npcs.notes: Ordering the elimination of all witnesses.

passive.name: Imperial Authority
passive.description: Lady Sinde commands Stormtroopers. Her orders are obeyed without question.

actions.name: Direct Fire
actions.description: Order a Stormtrooper to attack immediately.

actions.name: Cutting Assessment
actions.description: Social attack that dismantles cover stories. Targets Resist.

maneuver.name: Issue Orders
maneuver.description: One ally gains a bonus maneuver.

gambit.name: Ultimatum
gambit.description: Target must comply or be marked for elimination.

exploit.name: Call Reinforcements
exploit.description: Summon 4 Stormtrooper minions to her location.

threatBuild.notes: Commander. Resist 5 = Grit 3-1+2+1(disciplined). She is a logistics officer, not a frontline fighter. Her danger is the army behind her. (Recurring — same stat block as earlier scene.)

npcs.name: Stormtrooper Squad
npcs.notes: Securing the hangar. Professional and lethal.

passive.name: Disciplined Fire
passive.description: +1 Power on weapon attacks.

actions.name: E-11 Volley
actions.description: Ranged attack.

actions.name: Bayonet Strike
actions.description: Melee attack.

maneuver.name: Take Cover
maneuver.description: Move to cover. Gain [Cover 1].

exploit.name: Coordinated Fire
exploit.description: All Troopers on same target gain +1 Power.

threatBuild.notes: Standard Stormtrooper minions. Same as Adv1. Power 3. Defense 4. Masterful+ = takedown.

scenes.hazards: Open hangar bay. Tibanna gas crates (explosive if hit). Collapsing ceiling from Darga's escape. Stormtrooper crossfire.

decisions.choice: Fight to the ship and pursue Darga
decisions.consequence: Can get a trajectory lock on Darga's escape vector. Leads to Cloud City.

decisions.choice: Eliminate Lady Sinde and the Stormtroopers
decisions.consequence: Removes Imperial witnesses but costs time. Darga gets further away.

narrativeLinks.note: Darga flees to Cloud City, setting up the entire Adventure 3 pursuit.

skillChecks.context: Firefight across the hangar bay.
skillChecks.success: Accurate fire. Stormtroopers suppressed or eliminated.
skillChecks.failure: Pinned by return fire. [Suppressed] for 1 round.

skillChecks.context: Hot-wiring the heroes' ship or bypassing hangar controls.
skillChecks.success: Ship systems online. Ready for launch.
skillChecks.failure: Delayed launch. 2 additional rounds under fire.

skillChecks.context: Launching under heavy fire with Stormtroopers targeting the ship.
skillChecks.success: Clean launch. Break atmosphere.
skillChecks.failure: Ship takes hits during liftoff. Hull damage. Emergency repairs needed.

skillChecks.context: Sprinting across the open hangar to the ship.
skillChecks.success: Full speed. Reach the ship.
skillChecks.failure: Stumble or take fire. Slowed.

skillChecks.context: Tracking Darga's escape trajectory.
skillChecks.success: Partial trajectory lock. The vector aligns with the Anoat Sector. Bespin. Cloud City.
skillChecks.failure: Lost him. Must rely on other intel to find Darga.

encounters.name: Hangar Bay Firefight
encounters.description: Lady Sinde's Stormtroopers are eliminating witnesses. 8 troopers in firing positions behind crates.
encounters.tactics: Stormtroopers use Tibanna crates as cover. Shooting the crates causes explosions — area damage but clears positions.

environmentMechanics.name: Tibanna Gas Crates
environmentMechanics.effect: Explosive decompression. [Hazard 3] in the zone. Destroys cover.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Don't shoot near the crates. Or do — area denial works both ways.

environmentMechanics.name: Collapsing Ceiling
environmentMechanics.effect: Rubble blocks sections of the hangar. Difficult terrain. Some paths completely blocked.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Athletics to climb over. Tech to find alternate routes.

environmentMechanics.name: Open Air Hangar
environmentMechanics.effect: Takodana jungle visible below. Falls from the platform are survivable but result in a 2-scene delay.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Stay away from the edges.

rewards.narrative: Escape from Darga's fortress. Trajectory to Bespin/Cloud City. Denia rescued (or not). The adventure ends on the ship, in hyperspace, with Varth analyzing the trajectory data.

pacing.openingBeat: Burst through blast doors into the hangar. Darga's yacht is already lifting off.
pacing.risingAction: Stormtroopers open fire. Crates explode. Ceiling collapses.
pacing.climax: The sprint to the ship under fire.
pacing.resolution: Liftoff. Hyperspace. Varth's trajectory lock on Bespin.
